title: What is the best way/workaround to mirror a mirrored column?

posts.author: JackNagy (Jack Nagy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a Time Log board where I note which document I was working on at different times of the week.

These documents exist as items across a range of other boards.

Currently, when looking at the Time Log board, I am easily able to click my mirror column (connected to these other boards) and add a document in.

However, if I enter one of the document boards, that document will not have all the time entries related to it automatically linked — I need to manually tick the checkboxes for it.

I feel like I’m missing something really basic here; what’s the best way to automate this?

i.e. whenever Item 1 “linked” in a mirror column on another board, I want Item 2 to be automatically linked on Item 1’s home board in a mirror column.

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JackNagy

I’m not sure I completely understand what you’re looking to achieve here.

Are you looking to create a 2-way mirror (i.e. when Item 1 on Board A is connected to Item 1 on Board B, also create the connection on Board B back to Board A)?
If so, it is possible to create a 2-way link! You’ll just need to select this when you set up the Connect boards column.

image1557×949 146 KB

If you’re looking to automate your board connections so that items on your board are automatically linked to their respective items on another board, we do have a number of automations that you can use to create a logic for this.

You can find steps to set up both the 2-way connection and the automations in this article: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000635139-Connect-Boards-Column-Previously-Link-to-Item-Column-

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Best,

Dani

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JackNagy

Just wanted to check in here and see if you needed any more assistance with this?
We’d be happy to help

Best,

Dani
